title: Introduce Version Safety Advisor and retrograde-compatibility checks for upgrades/rollbacks
preview: Summary: Adds a new 'Version Safety Advisor' subsystem to the private installer that performs pre-deploy compatibility checks for upgrades and downgrades (retrograde checks), surface actionable incompatibility reports, and optionally block unsafe rollouts. Also introduces a timed restore lock to serialize restore op...
text:
Summary: Adds a new 'Version Safety Advisor' subsystem to the private installer that performs pre-deploy compatibility checks for upgrades and downgrades (retrograde checks), surface actionable incompatibility reports, and optionally block unsafe rollouts. Also introduces a timed restore lock to serialize restore operations, Helm chart validation hooks, and a Terraform module-level validation script.

Motivation: Past incidents (SUP-1342) showed latent schema incompatibilities and concurrent restore races during cluster upgrade windows. The existing installer provided atomic migration scripts but lacked a deterministic preflight that would catch mixing of incompatible manifests, deprecated CRDs, or database schema regressions when attempting a downgrade path. The goal is to reduce upgrade/rollback blast radius by surfacing problems before changes are applied and by preventing concurre...

title: sealedsecret: safe-swap flow with kms fallback, probe job, and recovery playbooks
preview: Motivation: private deployments that use SealedSecrets + Vault/KMS face a race during TLS/KMS key rotations where consumers may receive partially-rotated secrets. This PR implements a safe 'swap' flow to allow staged SealedSecret rotation with an automated compatibility probe and a documented rollback/recovery path....
text: Motivation: private deployments that use SealedSecrets + Vault/KMS face a race during TLS/KMS key rotations where consumers may receive partially-rotated secrets. This PR implements a safe 'swap' flow to allow staged SealedSecret rotation with an automated compatibility probe and a documented rollback/recovery path. Goals: 1) perform TLS/KMS-based rotation with zero-secret-latency windows for runtime; 2) detect keywrap/unwrap incompatibilities prior to cutover; 3) provide easy rollback and audit telemetry for operators. Changes are split across Helm templates, Helm hooks, a small controller-ish job for probing previously-rotated entries, Terraform variables for the installer, and runbooks/playbooks for operators. Checklist: - [x] Helm chart: new values for sealedsecret.swap.enabled, probeJob config, and pre-upgrade hook annotations - [x] Helm templates: introduce sealedsecret-safe-swa...

title: Add rollback dry-run emulator and Helm constraint enforcer
preview: Motivation: Upgrades for private deployments must have a low blast radius and the ability to verify a rollback without touching production state. This PR implements two complementary safety mechanisms: a rollback dry-run emulator (emulator) that simulates an in-place rollback sequence against an ephemeral clone of c...
text: Motivation: Upgrades for private deployments must have a low blast radius and the ability to verify a rollback without touching production state. This PR implements two complementary safety mechanisms: a rollback dry-run emulator (emulator) that simulates an in-place rollback sequence against an ephemeral clone of cluster state, and a Helm constraint enforcer that gates Helm value sets and chart versions during upgrade/rollback orchestration. Summary of changes: 1) installer/upgrade: add 'upgrade-dryrun-emulator' binary and operator API hooks. The emulator performs an automated sequence: snapshot export, ephemeral namespace restore, execute rollback steps (helm release rollback, CRD reduction/restore, DB migration reversal using migration runner in read-only mode), and post-run verification that checks service account bindings, KV cache compatibility, and storage layout. 2) operator: ...

title: hsm-keystore-sync-operator-add-rotation-plan-and-sealedsecret-migration
preview: Adds an idempotent HSM Keystore Sync Operator plus Helm and Terraform changes to automate certificate and keystore rotation for private deployments. Motivation: manual keystore rotation across on-prem HSMs and cloud KMS boundaries has been a high-friction operational task that caused outages during upgrades and prev...
text: Adds an idempotent HSM Keystore Sync Operator plus Helm and Terraform changes to automate certificate and keystore rotation for private deployments. Motivation: manual keystore rotation across on-prem HSMs and cloud KMS boundaries has been a high-friction operational task that caused outages during upgrades and prevented safe automated rollouts. This PR implements: 1) a lightweight operator (hsm-keystore-sync) that watches configured HSM instances and Vault/Transit endpoints, reconciles keystore/JKS/PKCS12 artifacts into a namespace-safe secret, and annotates resources with rotation metadata; 2) Helm pre-upgrade/post-upgrade hooks that perform staged keystore swaps and validate TLS handshake before switching live listeners; 3) a sealedsecret-to-vault-migration tool that converts selected SealedSecrets into Vault-backed secrets using an adoption plan and write-lock to avoid races; 4) T...

title: Add tenant snapshot coordinator and staged rollback gatekeeper
preview: Motivation: Private upgrades for multi-tenant on-prem deployments need lower blast radius and safer downgrade paths. This PR introduces a tenant-scoped SnapshotCoordinator CRD/controller and a staged rollback Gatekeeper that validates restore readiness and prevents accidental downgrades. The goal is to enable prefli...
text:
Motivation: Private upgrades for multi-tenant on-prem deployments need lower blast radius and safer downgrade paths. This PR introduces a tenant-scoped SnapshotCoordinator CRD/controller and a staged rollback Gatekeeper that validates restore readiness and prevents accidental downgrades. The goal is to enable preflight restore rehearsals and automatic gating during multi-tenant upgrades so operators can abort or stage rollbacks without impacting healthy tenants.

What this changes (high level):
- New CRD: TenantSnapshotRequest (api/v1alpha1) and controller that serializes snapshot creation per tenant, signs artifacts with release metadata, and records tenant-impact score.
- New controller: SnapshotCoordinator that can schedule rehearsal restores into an isolated test namespace (operator-managed) and produce a RestoreReadinessReport.
- Rollback gatekeeper: Operator-side admission webho...

title: add-schema-compatibility-checker-and-restore-validation
preview: Motivation: We observed multiple upgrade incidents where subtle schema changes (nullable -> not-null, removed columns, implicit type narrowing) caused partial upgrades and difficult rollbacks. This PR introduces an automated pre-upgrade compatibility check and a post-restore validation step to make private installer...
text:
Motivation: We observed multiple upgrade incidents where subtle schema changes (nullable -> not-null, removed columns, implicit type narrowing) caused partial upgrades and difficult rollbacks. This PR introduces an automated pre-upgrade compatibility check and a post-restore validation step to make private installer upgrades safer and more auditable.

What this PR does: Adds a schema compatibility checker that runs as part of the Helm pre-upgrade hook and the Terraform upgrade plan job. The checker compares the target release's migration manifest with a database introspection snapshot to detect incompatible operations (destructive drops, narrowing type changes, non-null constraints without defaults). If an incompatibility is detected, the hook fails the upgrade and emits diagnostic artifacts (schema-diff.json, sample rows) into the release-namespace PVC.

Backup/restore automation: In...

title: controlplane-subnet-failover-sentinel-and-route-priority-operator
preview: Context: Customers running Redwood Private across multi-AZ VPCs require deterministic and low-risk control-plane failover when an AZ becomes degraded. This PR introduces a failover sentinel and a route-priority operator that coordinate subnet handoff, ENI lease transfer, conditional peering/route adjustments, and a ...
text: Context: Customers running Redwood Private across multi-AZ VPCs require deterministic and low-risk control-plane failover when an AZ becomes degraded. This PR introduces a failover sentinel and a route-priority operator that coordinate subnet handoff, ENI lease transfer, conditional peering/route adjustments, and a preflight DNS cutover to minimize downtime and avoid split-brain. Motivation: Previous incidents showed manual subnet reallocation and DNS changes were error-prone and caused long recovery windows. We need an automated but controlled mechanism that integrates with the installer, Terraform modules, and Helm hooks so we can perform staged failovers with canary verification and easy rollback. Summary of changes: 1) New sentinel service (installer/cmd/sentinel) that monitors per-AZ ENI health, control-plane pod readiness, and VPC route table metrics; it evaluates failover condi...

title: manifest-diff-builder and KMS-stamped attestations for incremental private-installer releases
preview: Motivation: make private-installer releases incremental and cryptographically auditable without re-bundling full artifacts for every patch. This PR implements a manifest-diff builder along with KMS-stamped attestations for every delta to enable fast rollforward/rollback and compact provenance storage. Summary: add a...
text: Motivation: make private-installer releases incremental and cryptographically auditable without re-bundling full artifacts for every patch. This PR implements a manifest-diff builder along with KMS-stamped attestations for every delta to enable fast rollforward/rollback and compact provenance storage. Summary: add a new release tool 'manifest-diff' that computes a deterministic delta index between two installer builds, produce a compact delta tarball and an accompanying attest.json signed via customer-configured KMS (supports AWS KMS and Vault HSM). The pipeline integrates the diff artifacts into the release graph and updates release-manifests.yaml with delta entries and signature metadata. Commits summary: 1) initial manifest-diff implementation and tests; 2) KMS attestation client and integration layer; 3) CI pipeline changes to upload delta artifacts to release-bucket and verify at...

title: microk8s air-gapped runtime bundle, delta patcher, and Terraform provider fixes
preview: Motivation: customers running on-prem developer clusters requested an officially supported microk8s air-gapped installer path. This PR introduces a microk8s-targeted offline bundle, a delta-based patcher to dramatically reduce archive size for incremental updates, Terraform/provider fixes to surface registry mirror ...
text: Motivation: customers running on-prem developer clusters requested an officially supported microk8s air-gapped installer path. This PR introduces a microk8s-targeted offline bundle, a delta-based patcher to dramatically reduce archive size for incremental updates, Terraform/provider fixes to surface registry mirror and snap-specific hooks, and Helm chart adjustments so the runtime works consistently with microk8s networking and CRI defaults. Work includes: 1) new bundle layout under installer/airgap/bundles/microk8s with slim OCI layers and explicit snap hooks, 2) a delta_patcher service that computes binary deltas between bundle versions and an unpack-time apply step to keep download footprint small, 3) Terraform module changes to support microk8s node labeling, registry mirror variables, and a provider shim for the installer to write local registries, 4) Helm chart compatibility bit...

title: atomic manifest stitcher and airgap compatibility layer for distro-aware bootstrap
preview:
Title: Atomic manifest stitcher and airgap compatibility layer for distro-aware bootstrap.

Description: This PR introduces a new "manifest stitcher" tool and accompanying compatibility layer to simplify creating slim, atomic offline bundles that work across multiple Kubernetes distros (k3s, rke2, kubeadm). The stit...
text:
Title: Atomic manifest stitcher and airgap compatibility layer for distro-aware bootstrap.

Description: This PR introduces a new "manifest stitcher" tool and accompanying compatibility layer to simplify creating slim, atomic offline bundles that work across multiple Kubernetes distros (k3s, rke2, kubeadm). The stitcher consumes per-distro manifest fragments (CRDs, kubelet flags, CNI overlays) and outputs a single validated, signed and delta-friendly bundle that the private installer can apply atomically during bootstrap or upgrades. The changeset also includes Helm chart adjustments to support local-OCI registry overrides, a Terraform module to seed local registries with stitched OCI indices, and upgrade/rollback hooks to ensure safe application of delta updates in air-gapped environments.

Motivation and context: Customers deploying Redwood Private in air-gapped/on-prem environments...

title: add-auxkey-rotation-controller-and-cert-proxy-helm-terraform-integration
preview: Motivation: Private installer customers asked for a safer, auditable way to rotate signing keys stored in HSMs and to automate certificate refreshes without application downtime. This PR implements an auxkey rotation controller that orchestrates staged HSM key rollovers, integrates with Vault transit (or PKCS11 plug...
text:
Motivation: Private installer customers asked for a safer, auditable way to rotate signing keys stored in HSMs and to automate certificate refreshes without application downtime. This PR implements an auxkey rotation controller that orchestrates staged HSM key rollovers, integrates with Vault transit (or PKCS11 plugins), and provides Helm + Terraform primitives and upgrade hooks so operators can adopt automated rotations with rollback safety.

What this PR does (high level):
- Adds `signing-auxkey-controller` binary (k8s controller) that: performs staged key generation, publishes new public material to a configurable endpoint, triggers certificate reissue via the cert-proxy sidecar, and performs a coordinated switchover once signatures verify.
- Helm chart changes: a new `cert-proxy` sidecar template, pre/postupgrade hooks for key-sync and cache-warm, and a `postrender` helper to inje...

title: sealed-bundle-registry-hash-rotation-and-helm-overlay-transformer
preview: Motivation: Improve on-prem sealed-bundle upgrades in air-gapped environments by enabling atomic registry hash rotation and providing a Helm overlay transformer that rewrites chart image references to a local seeded registry. This addresses two common failure modes: (1) operator mismatch when bundle image digests ch...
text:
Motivation: Improve on-prem sealed-bundle upgrades in air-gapped environments by enabling atomic registry hash rotation and providing a Helm overlay transformer that rewrites chart image references to a local seeded registry. This addresses two common failure modes: (1) operator mismatch when bundle image digests change during incremental upgrades, and (2) manual, error-prone sed-based image rewrites during preseed operations.

What this PR does: Adds a registry hash rotation mechanism to the offline bundle layout and a Helm overlay transformer used by the installer preflight and apply phases. The registry rotation ensures sealed-bundle manifests and OCI index entries can be remapped atomically to a new local registry prefix using a rotation map file. The Helm overlay transformer is a small, deterministic engine that rewrites Helm values/image fields and Chart.lock entries to point at...

title: prevent-downgrades-and-standardize-on-disk-backup-format
preview: Motivation: Past upgrades have exposed two recurring problems for private deployments: accidental downgrades (operator error or rollback scripts that do not account for schema/feature flags) and inconsistent backup formats across installer versions which makes restores brittle. This PR introduces a lightweight downg...
text:
Motivation: Past upgrades have exposed two recurring problems for private deployments: accidental downgrades (operator error or rollback scripts that do not account for schema/feature flags) and inconsistent backup formats across installer versions which makes restores brittle. This PR introduces a lightweight downgrade-blocker in the installer, pins on-disk backup metadata to a stable v2 format, and adds a deterministic restore test to the CI matrix.

Summary of changes: Added a check in the private installer that refuses to apply a release with an older semantic version if that release would require a schema downgrade; the check is driven by a new release-metadata.json (version + schema_version + migration_path). Implemented a stable on-disk backup v2 format (single tar.gz with manifests/etcd-snapshots/secrets.enc and a compact manifest.json) and utilities to produce/consume it. Int...

title: automation: multi-AZ failover + restore-drill runbooks-as-code for private installer
preview: Motivation: Private customer deployments (VPC/on-prem) need automated, testable HA and DR runbooks. This PR introduces orchestration and configuration changes that enable multi-AZ deployments, automated failover testing (restore drills), and runbooks-as-code so runbooks can be versioned and executed by the installer...
text:
Motivation: Private customer deployments (VPC/on-prem) need automated, testable HA and DR runbooks. This PR introduces orchestration and configuration changes that enable multi-AZ deployments, automated failover testing (restore drills), and runbooks-as-code so runbooks can be versioned and executed by the installer.

What this PR does: Adds a reusable Terraform module for multi-AZ subnet groups and load-balancer failover configuration; extends the private-installer CLI with a --drill-runbook flag to execute runbooks-as-code against a cluster; introduces Helm values to control zone affinity and priority-based pod scheduling for graceful cross-AZ failover; and adds YAML runbook templates with step-by-step, idempotent tasks to run restore drills and validate recovery.

Design notes: The Terraform module uses provider-aware subnet mapping to ensure each AZ gets isolated subnets and a cro...

title: add kubeadm vendor bundle + local registry-proxy for air-gapped installs
preview: Motivation: Customers running on-prem with kubeadm and strict air-gap constraints requested a self-contained vendor bundle and a small registry-proxy to bootstrap image syncs from operator media. This PR introduces a kubeadm-targeted offline bundle layout, a lightweight registry-proxy that serves the bundled images ...
text: Motivation: Customers running on-prem with kubeadm and strict air-gap constraints requested a self-contained vendor bundle and a small registry-proxy to bootstrap image syncs from operator media. This PR introduces a kubeadm-targeted offline bundle layout, a lightweight registry-proxy that serves the bundled images locally and a set of install-time validators and checksums. The changes aim to reduce manual steps for operators and provide a repeatable install/upgrade flow for kubeadm clusters in disconnected environments. High level changes: 1) bundles/kubeadm/: new bundle layout with images/, manifests/, checksums.txt and a manifest-catalog.json used by the installer to map images to components. 2) installer/airgap/registry-proxy: Dockerfile + minimal Go-based proxy that exposes a small HTTP API to list available images and perform staged pulls into the cluster registry (supports TLS ...

title: add offline distro image-indexing + delta snapshot tooling for safe on-prem upgrades
preview: Motivation: customers running air-gapped/private deployments require deterministic offline upgrade artifacts and a compact index that maps distro variants and kube runtimes to vendored OCI images. This PR adds a stand-alone image indexer and delta snapshot generator to the offline installer toolchain so operators ca...
text: Motivation: customers running air-gapped/private deployments require deterministic offline upgrade artifacts and a compact index that maps distro variants and kube runtimes to vendored OCI images. This PR adds a stand-alone image indexer and delta snapshot generator to the offline installer toolchain so operators can produce minimal upgrade bundles and validated rollback snapshots without access to public registries. Summary: implements an image indexing service (cli and library), a delta snapshot format (signed manifest + OCI manifest refs), Helm chart changes to consume indexed manifests at install/upgrade time, Terraform module updates for offline bundle storage metadata, and a small orchestration helper that validates rollback compatibility before applying upgrades. Design: image-indexer scans a local tarball of mirrored OCI layers or a registry mirror endpoint and emits a compact...

title: staged-rollback-with-dependency-graph-and-state-seed-preservation
preview: Context: customers running Private deployments reported rare failures when performing multi-stage upgrades where component inter-dependencies (schema, config, seed data) required coordinated rollbacks. Motivation: provide a safe, testable staged rollback path that understands service dependency ordering and preserve...
text: Context: customers running Private deployments reported rare failures when performing multi-stage upgrades where component inter-dependencies (schema, config, seed data) required coordinated rollbacks. Motivation: provide a safe, testable staged rollback path that understands service dependency ordering and preserves tenant-visible seeded state so that partial rollbacks do not leave the cluster in an inconsistent or unbootable condition. What this PR does: 1) Introduces a reversible dependency graph used by the installer to compute rollback stages; edges cover DB migrations, config map velocity, helm chart ordering, and tenant seed/data artifacts. 2) Adds ‘seed-preserve’ mode: when enabled, pre-rollback hooks snapshot tenant-seeded objects (application-level seeds and lightweight KV entries) into encrypted, versioned manifests stored in object storage with a TTL and rotation policy. 3...

title: Control-plane subnet affinity policy and conditional DNS proxying for cross-VPC control plane
preview: Context: Customers running Redwood Private across multiple VPCs and accounts have seen control-plane flakiness when control-plane endpoints and control-plane instances are scattered across AZs and when cross-VPC DNS forwarding is not consistently available. This PR implements a deterministic affinity policy for allo...
text: Context: Customers running Redwood Private across multiple VPCs and accounts have seen control-plane flakiness when control-plane endpoints and control-plane instances are scattered across AZs and when cross-VPC DNS forwarding is not consistently available. This PR implements a deterministic affinity policy for allocating control-plane subnets (prefer stickiness to preferred AZs and capacity pools) and adds a conditional DNS proxying helper that automates resolver handoff for hosted/private route scenarios. Motivation: reduce cross-AZ control-plane chattiness, avoid hot-spotting of ephemeral endpoints during rolling upgrades, and make DNS handoff deterministic for cross-account control-plane peering. What changed (high level): 1) Introduce a subnet-affinity policy module used by the installer to pick or create control-plane subnets based on zone scores, capacity tags, and historical a...

title: split-airgap-values-and-delegated-init-wave-controller
preview: Motivation: air-gapped/private installs have several implicit assumptions in the existing values.yaml templates: image pre-pull behavior, init job sequencing, and hook ownership are entangled with a single values tree which makes upgrades and rollbacks fragile when offline registries or signing/verification steps ar...
text: Motivation: air-gapped/private installs have several implicit assumptions in the existing values.yaml templates: image pre-pull behavior, init job sequencing, and hook ownership are entangled with a single values tree which makes upgrades and rollbacks fragile when offline registries or signing/verification steps are required. This PR separates the airgap-specific value overlays from the core init sequencing templates and introduces a delegated init-wave controller that can be toggled per-tenant. Summary of changes: 1) Add values schema: chart/values-airgap.schema.json (new overlay set) and update values.schema.json with a top-level 'airgap' overlay pointer. 2) Factor templates: chart/templates/init-waves-delegated.yaml (new controller-backed wave template), chart/templates/init-waves-legacy.yaml (backward-compatible path), chart/templates/airgap-prepull-daemonset.yaml (optional, gate...

title: Introduce key-sink operator and upgrade-safe TLS signer orchestration for private installer
preview: Problem: Upgrading the private installer in environments that use an external KMS/HSM and a cluster-based TLS signer often required manual steps: draining signer keys, resealing/unsealing Vault, and coordinating certificate signer swaps. This caused support churn for on-prem upgrades and blocked some air-gapped inst...
text:
Problem: Upgrading the private installer in environments that use an external KMS/HSM and a cluster-based TLS signer often required manual steps: draining signer keys, resealing/unsealing Vault, and coordinating certificate signer swaps. This caused support churn for on-prem upgrades and blocked some air-gapped installs.

Goal: Introduce a light-weight 'key-sink' operator that can absorb signer key handoffs, provide a predictable handover API during chart upgrades, and add upgrade/rollback hooks (Helm + Terraform) so the installer can perform non-disruptive TLS signer rotations and Vault unseal orchestration. The implementation must be idempotent and safe to re-run for interrupted upgrades.

What this PR does: Adds a Kubernetes operator (key-sink) into the private-installer distribution that watches for SignerHandOff custom resources and atomically persists ephemeral signer material t...

title: HSM Shield operator: staged key promotion + TLS signer + graceful Helm upgrade hooks
preview: Problem / Context: Customers running Private deployments in air-gapped or regulated datacenters require safe, auditable HSM key rotations and certificate replacements that can be performed with zero-downtime and an automated rollback path. Existing tooling handled key promotion but lacked an orchestrated, Helm-nativ...
text:
Problem / Context: Customers running Private deployments in air-gapped or regulated datacenters require safe, auditable HSM key rotations and certificate replacements that can be performed with zero-downtime and an automated rollback path. Existing tooling handled key promotion but lacked an orchestrated, Helm-native flow for staged promotion, a local TLS signing shim for offline environments, and Terraform primitives to bind policy and grants consistently across upgrades.

This PR introduces the 'HSM Shield' operator and several installer surface changes to provide: staged key promotion (canary -> staged -> promoted) with automatic traffic fencing, a TLS signing adapter that can run in air-gapped clusters and perform CSR approvals via a controlled HSM signing path, Helm post-upgrade/rollback hooks to ensure safe cert churn during chart upgrades, and a small Terraform module to bind o...

title: staged-egress-nat-allocator-and-conditional-dns-delegation-rollback
preview: Motivation: customer on-prem control planes need deterministic AZ-aware egress NAT allocation and an auditable, reversible handoff of Route53 private delegation for cross-account resolver setups. This PR introduces a Terraform module and installer hooks to provision a staged egress NAT allocator with zone affinity, ...
text: Motivation: customer on-prem control planes need deterministic AZ-aware egress NAT allocation and an auditable, reversible handoff of Route53 private delegation for cross-account resolver setups. This PR introduces a Terraform module and installer hooks to provision a staged egress NAT allocator with zone affinity, ephemeral ENI support for control plane endpoints, and a conditional DNS delegation workflow that can be rolled back safely. Summary of changes: 1) terraform/modules/egress-nat-allocator: new module that computes AZ affinity, allocates NAT Gateways or ENI-based NAT proxies based on deployment flavor, and exposes a staged allocation plan for rollback. 2) terraform/modules/controlplane-subnet: add tags and ownership metadata to enable deterministic subnet selection by allocator. 3) installer/hooks/preflight and installer/hooks/post-rollout: add scripted handoff steps to creat...

title: Add offline Helm OCI seeding and precompiled BPF-CNI module bundles for air-gapped kernels
preview: Motivation: Customers running Redwood Private in fully air-gapped datacenters reported two recurring issues: 1) inconsistent CNI installs when kernels differ from our build matrix, and 2) manual, error-prone steps to seed private OCI/Helm registries. This PR introduces an end-to-end offline-friendly flow: packaged, ...
text: Motivation: Customers running Redwood Private in fully air-gapped datacenters reported two recurring issues: 1) inconsistent CNI installs when kernels differ from our build matrix, and 2) manual, error-prone steps to seed private OCI/Helm registries. This PR introduces an end-to-end offline-friendly flow: packaged, precompiled BPF-CNI kernel module bundles for common enterprise kernels plus a new OCI-Helm seeder sidecar utility that can be run from the airgap bootstrap container to atomically seed registries and charts. Summary of changes: packaged kernel module artifacts per distro (ubuntu20/22, rhel8/9, rocky, almalinux) with vmlinuz/headers checksums; an installer hook that probes target nodes and selects the closest-compatible module bundle; an offline Helm OCI seeder that pulls from provided artifact tarballs and imports images/charts into the local registry with optional signatu...

title: installer: tenant-zone CMK placement + installer-signed-audit-envelopes
preview: Background: customers running private/on-prem deployments requested stronger guarantees that customer-managed keys (CMKs) are provisioned and used only within their configured data zone and that audit events emitted by the installer are tamper-evident for compliance audits. This PR adds installer-side enforcement of...
text: Background: customers running private/on-prem deployments requested stronger guarantees that customer-managed keys (CMKs) are provisioned and used only within their configured data zone and that audit events emitted by the installer are tamper-evident for compliance audits. This PR adds installer-side enforcement of CMK placement (zone mapping) and an optional installer audit-envelope signer that produces a signed, immutable wrapper for audit batches so customers can retain a verifiable trail outside the control plane. Scope: - Preflight and onboarding validation for tenant->zone CMK mapping (installer preflight fails if CMK references a different region/zone) - Terraform + Helm changes to surface a tenant_kms_map var that binds tenant identifiers to allowed KMS zones - Installer KMS affinity controller that ensures API calls for key usage are routed only to allowed endpoints and reje...

title: add-transactional-tenant-seed-controller-and-quiesce-hook-values
preview: Context and motivation: Private installer customers have hit flaky tenant seed runs and race conditions during rolling upgrades when tenant bootstrap jobs are requeued or retried. This PR introduces a values-first approach that wires an opinionated, idempotent tenant-seed controller configuration and a pluggable qui...
text: Context and motivation: Private installer customers have hit flaky tenant seed runs and race conditions during rolling upgrades when tenant bootstrap jobs are requeued or retried. This PR introduces a values-first approach that wires an opinionated, idempotent tenant-seed controller configuration and a pluggable quiesce-hook scaffold so operators can opt into safe quiesce semantics during upgrades. Goals: (1) Provide a values schema for transactional seed semantics (lease, idempotency, backoff profiles), (2) Add templated quiesce hook that can be enabled per-tenant or cluster-wide, (3) Surface init telemetry toggles so the console and observability stack can correlate seed progress across retries. Changes are behind feature gates and default to backward-compatible behavior. Checklist: - [x] values.schema.json for charts/redwood-private with new top-level keys: seed.transactional, seed...

title: introduce-sealedsecret-snapshot-rotator-with-transit-rekey-and-helm-upgrade-hooks
preview: Context and motivation: Private deployments need an auditable, rollback-safe way to rotate secrets and keep recoverable backups of SealedSecrets when performing upgrades or rekey operations. Existing sealedsecret workflows at customers relied on ad-hoc scripts that are error-prone during multi-cluster upgrades and H...
text: Context and motivation: Private deployments need an auditable, rollback-safe way to rotate secrets and keep recoverable backups of SealedSecrets when performing upgrades or rekey operations. Existing sealedsecret workflows at customers relied on ad-hoc scripts that are error-prone during multi-cluster upgrades and HSM/KMS key rotations. This PR introduces a controller and operational tooling that: 1) periodically snapshots SealedSecrets into a versioned encrypted archive, 2) stores snapshots in a user-configurable backend (S3-compatible or internal object store) encrypted with a transit KMS (Vault/AWS KMS) envelope, and 3) supports staged rekey/promotion with a safe rollback path. The design emphasizes minimal blast radius: snapshots are immutable objects with metadata linking to the KMS key version that encrypted them. Highlights and checklist: - New controller: sealedsecret-snapshot...

title: Add mixed-version cluster compatibility and stitched-restore plan for cross-version migrations
preview: Motivation: Customers running private deployments often end up with mixed-version clusters during rolling upgrades, partial rollouts, or emergency hotfixes. Existing restore tooling assumes homogeneous cluster versions which leads to failed restores or irreversible schema changes. This PR introduces a lightweight co...
text:
Motivation: Customers running private deployments often end up with mixed-version clusters during rolling upgrades, partial rollouts, or emergency hotfixes. Existing restore tooling assumes homogeneous cluster versions which leads to failed restores or irreversible schema changes. This PR introduces a lightweight compatibility shim plus a new 'stitched restore' workflow that can perform deterministic, reversible restores across mixed-version clusters without requiring full cluster-wide upgrades beforehand.

What's included: 1) compatibility shim: operator-side probe and per-node capability matrix (version_probe.go) that detects which nodes accept forward/backward compatible migrations and which require guarded operations. 2) stitched-restore executor: an orchestrator (stitched_restore.go) that constructs a deterministic sequence of migration and replay steps, splitting large migration...

title: hook-priority-scheduler-and-template-dependency-resolution
preview: Introduce a hook priority scheduler and deterministic template dependency resolution for the private installer Helm charts and hook jobs. Motivation: tenants with large numbers of tenant-scoped init hooks were occasionally triggering concurrent heavy migrations and image pulls that caused network/registry rate limit...
text: Introduce a hook priority scheduler and deterministic template dependency resolution for the private installer Helm charts and hook jobs. Motivation: tenants with large numbers of tenant-scoped init hooks were occasionally triggering concurrent heavy migrations and image pulls that caused network/registry rate limits and prolonged upgrade windows. This PR adds (1) a scheduler that assigns hook execution windows and priority buckets to init hooks via values.yaml configuration, (2) deterministic template dependency graph resolution so hooks declared with 'requires' ordering are applied consistently across helm renders and upgrades, and (3) an optional init-rate-limiter sidecar controller integration that coordinates cross-tenant concurrency using a ConfigMap-backed lease. The change includes: - new values schema fields (hookGroups, priorityBuckets, templateDeps, rateLimiter) with valida...

title: add encrypted ENI bootstrap flow and deterministic DNS mirror rollback hooks
preview: Context: Customers deploying Private control planes in strict environments asked for stronger network-level guarantees: (1) control-plane ENIs must be provisioned encrypted at boot and bound to predictable IP allocations so firewall rules and egress policies can be pre-provisioned, and (2) DNS mirroring used during ...
text: Context: Customers deploying Private control planes in strict environments asked for stronger network-level guarantees: (1) control-plane ENIs must be provisioned encrypted at boot and bound to predictable IP allocations so firewall rules and egress policies can be pre-provisioned, and (2) DNS mirroring used during control-plane upgrades must support a safe rollback path that avoids left-behind stub zones or split-horizon drift. This PR implements an encrypted ENI bootstrapping flow in the Terraform installer, adds a deterministic IP assignment mode for controlplane subnets, introduces a lightweight Helm chart hook to seed a DNS mirror with signed zone metadata, and wires an automated rollback hook that re-scopes stub zone records and removes ephemeral mirror records on failed upgrades. Scope: terraform/modules/network/controlplane/* - new encrypted_eni module and deterministic_ip sub...

title: Emit audit events during Private install/upgrade/rollback/config apply
preview: Context: Private customers reported gaps in audit coverage for operator-driven installer actions (notably upgrades and RBAC-related config applies) and Security needs parity with Hosted/Dedicated for “sensitive actions.” This PR instruments the Private installer to emit audit events for install/upgrade/rollback/conf...
text: Context: Private customers reported gaps in audit coverage for operator-driven installer actions (notably upgrades and RBAC-related config applies) and Security needs parity with Hosted/Dedicated for “sensitive actions.” This PR instruments the Private installer to emit audit events for install/upgrade/rollback/config-apply/credential-rotation flows using the audit schema v1 envelope and event_type taxonomy. Summary of changes: (1) Add an audit emitter to the installer runtime that writes v1-shaped events to the local audit spool and forwards to the cluster audit ingestion endpoint when configured. (2) Emit events at the start + completion of sensitive workflows with consistent correlation (admin_action_id) and stable event_id for dedupe. (3) Capture operator identity where available (kubeconfig user / OIDC subject / OS user) without logging secrets. (4) Include deployment context: pr...
links: Jira SUP-18421 (Private audit logs missing RBAC events)
Confluence: Audit Log Schema v1 + Sensitive Actions taxonomy (referenced in docs/audit-logging.md)

title: Add upgrade diagnostics bundle + failure hints for stuck migrations
preview: Context: Support escalations for Private upgrades getting stuck at migrations are taking too long to triage because we’re missing a consistent artifact set and the installer’s failure output doesn’t guide the operator to the right next step. This PR adds a first-class diagnostics bundle and improves upgrade/migratio...
text:
Context: Support escalations for Private upgrades getting stuck at migrations are taking too long to triage because we’re missing a consistent artifact set and the installer’s failure output doesn’t guide the operator to the right next step. This PR adds a first-class diagnostics bundle and improves upgrade/migrations failure hints so Support/SRE/Platform can move from “stuck” -> “actionable” quickly. Ties to SUP-1845 and ENG-4828.

What’s included:
- New command: `redwood-private-installer diagnostics bundle --reason upgrade --output <path>`
 - Produces a single `.tar.gz` with a predictable directory structure.
 - Explicitly avoids collecting secrets (no K8s Secrets, no env dumps from pods). Basic redaction is applied to common token patterns in logs.
 - Includes a `MANIFEST.txt` with versions and timestamps to make email/Slack handoffs easier.
- Upgrade/migrations aware collection:
...

title: airgap: add apt/yum repo metadata + slim runtime bundles for offline installs
preview: Motivation: customers running on-prem/private installs often hit two pain points: (1) large monolithic tarballs that waste bandwidth and storage when only runtime artifacts are needed; (2) lack of standard apt/yum metadata in bundles makes tooling brittle for distributions that expect repo indices. This PR introduce...
text: Motivation: customers running on-prem/private installs often hit two pain points: (1) large monolithic tarballs that waste bandwidth and storage when only runtime artifacts are needed; (2) lack of standard apt/yum metadata in bundles makes tooling brittle for distributions that expect repo indices. This PR introduces a new bundle layout and tooling to reduce airgap bundle size, support OS package repositories out-of-the-box, and improve reproducible installs. Summary of changes: add airgap repo metadata generator that creates Packages.gz and repodata/repomd.xml for deb/rpm artifacts; add lightweight 'slim' runtime images for init, health-probe and migrator containers (alpine-based with static-linked helpers) and a build pipeline that outputs both full and slim images into the bundle; introduce an 'airgap-manifest.json' (minimal, stable schema v1) that lists package names, shasums, sig...

title: Private installer: add safe optimization config rollback command (pin + verify)
preview:
Context
Private deployments now support versioned Optimization Configs with safe apply + one-click rollback in Console. Private operators requested an equivalent workflow when Console access is unavailable (break-glass) or when rolling back during cluster/network issues.

This PR adds an operator-friendly rollback c...
text:
Context
Private deployments now support versioned Optimization Configs with safe apply + one-click rollback in Console. Private operators requested an equivalent workflow when Console access is unavailable (break-glass) or when rolling back during cluster/network issues.

This PR adds an operator-friendly rollback command to the `redwood-private-installer` CLI. The workflow is designed to be safe by default (explicit scope + confirmation), idempotent (retries won’t wedge state), and observable (structured logs + correlation IDs).

What’s in this PR
- New CLI command: `rwpi optimize-config rollback`
 - Rolls back the active Optimization Config version for a given scope by calling the Private control plane rollback API.
 - Supports explicit targeting:
 - `--tenant <tenant_slug>` (required)
 - `--model <model_id>` (optional; defaults to tenant-wide if omitted)
 - `--region <region>` (opt...

title: deterministic-gpu-nodepool-stencil-tfmodule-checkpoint
preview: Context & motivation: Private deployments require predictable nodepool identities and safer rollback semantics when operators apply changes across air-gapped or peered VPCs. Existing modules create GPU nodepools with provider-generated names and rely on global tfstate only; this makes cross-account reconciliation an...
text: Context & motivation: Private deployments require predictable nodepool identities and safer rollback semantics when operators apply changes across air-gapped or peered VPCs. Existing modules create GPU nodepools with provider-generated names and rely on global tfstate only; this makes cross-account reconciliation and on-prem rollback brittle. This PR introduces a new terraform module (modules/gpu-nodepool-stencil) that provides deterministic naming, optional lease-backed ephemeral IAM bindings, and a lightweight tfstate checkpointing mechanism anchored to network tags. Goals: 1) deterministic, stable nodepool identifiers to allow safe rebinds and tenant moves; 2) lease-based IAM sessions so nodepools get least-privilege, short-lived credentials for cluster bootstrap; 3) checkpointed state objects (S3/GCS) tied to VPC/subnet tags to support orchestrated rollbacks and faster drift detec...

title: Add break-glass flag for bundle verification bypass + explicit audit warning
preview: Context: We now fail-closed on offline bundle signature/manifest verification during preflight. For a small set of air-gapped environments, we still need an emergency, explicitly-audited path to proceed when verification cannot be performed (e.g., corrupted trust store update package, temporary time source issues, o...
text:
Context: We now fail-closed on offline bundle signature/manifest verification during preflight. For a small set of air-gapped environments, we still need an emergency, explicitly-audited path to proceed when verification cannot be performed (e.g., corrupted trust store update package, temporary time source issues, or operational incident). This PR introduces a break-glass override flag that is policy-gated and leaves strong evidence (console warnings + local audit markers) when used.

What this PR does: Adds a break-glass path for preflight/install that allows skipping bundle signature verification only when an explicit local policy allows it. The override is intentionally noisy and requires operators to provide a reason + approval reference, and we emit audit events for both attempted and successful usage.

CLI / UX changes: - New flag: --break-glass-skip-bundle-verification (alias: ...

title: Docs: update air-gapped installation flow for signed offline bundles
preview: Summary: Updates the in-repo installer documentation to reflect the new signed offline bundle workflow used for air-gapped installs. This PR is intentionally focused on README/docs content and examples (no behavior changes), and aligns terminology with recent installer work: bundle manifest, detached signature, trus...
text:
Summary: Updates the in-repo installer documentation to reflect the new signed offline bundle workflow used for air-gapped installs. This PR is intentionally focused on README/docs content and examples (no behavior changes), and aligns terminology with recent installer work: bundle manifest, detached signature, trust store, preflight verification, downgrade prevention, and break-glass override logging.

Why: Pilot customers and Support/SEs were following the older “copy bundle + run install” flow and missing the required verification step. In addition, the new tooling (bundle inspect/verify) and the fail-closed behavior in preflight needs to be documented with the exact command lines and expected outputs so air-gapped operators can self-diagnose issues like hash mismatch, unknown key, and time skew.

What changed (docs only): 1) Rewrote the Air-gapped Installation section to make veri...

title: local-registry-fast-sync-and-rollback-snapshots
preview: Motivation: Private on-prem installs frequently hit long bootstrap windows when seeding large OCI registries over slow links. This PR introduces a delta-aware fast-sync engine plus snapshot-based rollback anchors so customers can perform atomic upgrades and quickly revert to a known registry state in air-gapped or V...
text: Motivation: Private on-prem installs frequently hit long bootstrap windows when seeding large OCI registries over slow links. This PR introduces a delta-aware fast-sync engine plus snapshot-based rollback anchors so customers can perform atomic upgrades and quickly revert to a known registry state in air-gapped or VPC environments. What changed: 1) new Terraform module redwood.local_registry that orchestrates an initial seeding plan and creates a signed snapshot manifest, 2) a sync agent (installer/bin/registry-sync) that performs checksum-driven delta pulls, resumes interrupted transfers, and applies layer-level parallelism tuned for low-latency paper networks, 3) snapshot manifest format and a lightweight anchor scheme (snap-<epoch>-<seq>) stored in the registry under a signed tag, 4) Helm chart additions: registry-mirror-init sidecar, offline-values configmap templating and a prefl...

title: Prevent offline bundle downgrade using signed manifest version + local install state
preview:
## Summary
This PR adds downgrade protection for Redwood Private offline bundles by enforcing monotonic install versions based on signed manifest metadata and a local install state record. After signature verification succeeds, the installer compares the bundle's signed version against the last successfully installe...
text:
## Summary
This PR adds downgrade protection for Redwood Private offline bundles by enforcing monotonic install versions based on signed manifest metadata and a local install state record. After signature verification succeeds, the installer compares the bundle's signed version against the last successfully installed version recorded on disk; if the incoming bundle is older, preflight fails closed with an actionable error.

## Why
Air-gapped customers frequently move bundles around via removable media. Without downgrade protection, an attacker (or an operational mistake) can replay an older-but-valid signed bundle to reintroduce known vulnerabilities or regress critical fixes. Because our threat model includes replay/downgrade as a realistic risk in offline workflows, we need the installer to defend against accidental or malicious rollbacks by default.

## Design
- New signed manifest...

title: Add SBOM pointer + sha256 to offline bundle manifest and verify offline
preview: Context: Air-gapped customers are asking for a verifiable SBOM as part of the offline bundle so they can do internal intake (security scanning / procurement) without relying on external links. We already sign the manifest and verify artifact hashes; this PR extends the manifest to reference the SBOM artifact(s) and ...
text: Context: Air-gapped customers are asking for a verifiable SBOM as part of the offline bundle so they can do internal intake (security scanning / procurement) without relying on external links. We already sign the manifest and verify artifact hashes; this PR extends the manifest to reference the SBOM artifact(s) and verifies the SBOM file hash offline during preflight. Summary of changes: (1) Manifest schema: adds an optional `sbom` entry with fields for `uri` (relative path in bundle or file:// URI), `sha256` (hex), and `format` (e.g., spdx-json, cyclonedx-json). The SBOM entry is included in the signed manifest payload, so tampering is detected via signature verification and also via explicit hash verification. (2) Bundle layout convention: recommends placing SBOMs under `sbom/` in the bundle root (e.g., `sbom/redwood-private-<version>.spdx.json`). The manifest stores the relative pa...

title: Wire signed offline bundle metadata into generated support bundle
preview: Context: With signed offline bundles, Support/SEs frequently need to answer "did verification run" and "what exactly was verified" from a support bundle alone (esp. in air-gapped environments where customers share a tarball via offline transport). Today the support bundle includes installer logs and config, but it d...
text: Context: With signed offline bundles, Support/SEs frequently need to answer "did verification run" and "what exactly was verified" from a support bundle alone (esp. in air-gapped environments where customers share a tarball via offline transport). Today the support bundle includes installer logs and config, but it does not summarize the offline bundle’s manifest/signature state, leading to longer back-and-forth (SUP-31844) and mis-triage when transport corrupts artifacts (SUP-31892). This PR adds a structured summary file and a small amount of diagnostic output so that a support bundle can stand alone for first-pass triage without exposing sensitive customer data. What this changes: (1) Adds a new support bundle attachment: support/bundle-verification.json containing the last-known offline bundle verification result, plus key metadata pulled from the signed manifest and verifier. (2) ...

title: Improve offline bundle verification error messages + remediation hints
preview: Context: With signed offline bundles, verification failures are expected to be one of the top support drivers during pilot. Today, failures often surface as generic crypto/library errors with no guidance on what to do next, and logs are not consistently referenced in the CLI output. This PR keeps fail-closed behavio...
text:
Context: With signed offline bundles, verification failures are expected to be one of the top support drivers during pilot. Today, failures often surface as generic crypto/library errors with no guidance on what to do next, and logs are not consistently referenced in the CLI output. This PR keeps fail-closed behavior unchanged, but makes the UX/support story significantly better.

What this changes: - Introduces a structured error taxonomy for bundle verification with stable error codes (RV-VERIFY-0xxx). - Maps common failure modes to a short 'what happened' + 'what to do next' remediation block, including where to find logs. - Adds a single-line summary suitable for copy/paste into tickets, plus a verbose output behind --verbose. - Normalizes log locations across Linux packages and containerized installer runs, and prints the resolved log path in the error footer. - Adds a 'transport...

title: Add integration tests for tampered offline bundles (fail closed)
preview: Context: As part of offline bundle signing + verification, we need high-signal integration tests that simulate real customer failure modes (tampering during transfer, swapped artifacts, partial copies) and ensure the installer preflight fails closed with actionable errors. This PR adds an integration test suite that...
text: Context: As part of offline bundle signing + verification, we need high-signal integration tests that simulate real customer failure modes (tampering during transfer, swapped artifacts, partial copies) and ensure the installer preflight fails closed with actionable errors. This PR adds an integration test suite that generates/uses a minimal signed test bundle fixture and then applies targeted corruption steps: (1) modified manifest contents (JSON canonicalization changes + byte edits), (2) swapped artifact file while leaving manifest unchanged, (3) truncated bundle archive (simulating partial rsync/usb copy), and (4) missing signature file for detached layout. Each scenario asserts that `installer preflight` exits non-zero, emits the expected error code, and does not proceed to any install step. The suite also verifies that the error taxonomy remains stable (Support can map failures) ...

title: Refactor bundle download-disabled paths (air-gapped mode never fetches missing artifacts)
preview: Context: We’ve had multiple pilot/poc environments where outbound egress is hard-disabled (or DNS is blackholed), but the installer still *attempts* to fetch “helpful” missing artifacts (chart tarballs, index.yaml, image manifests) when an offline bundle is incomplete or the user points at the wrong bundle path. In ...
text:
Context: We’ve had multiple pilot/poc environments where outbound egress is hard-disabled (or DNS is blackholed), but the installer still *attempts* to fetch “helpful” missing artifacts (chart tarballs, index.yaml, image manifests) when an offline bundle is incomplete or the user points at the wrong bundle path. In air-gapped installs this produces long timeouts and confusing errors, and more importantly violates the expectation that air-gapped mode never reaches out to the network.

This PR hardens the download-disabled paths by (1) centralizing network policy enforcement and (2) making missing-offline-artifact handling fail-fast with actionable remediation.

What changed: - Introduced a single NetworkPolicy gate (AllowOutbound|DenyOutbound) wired into all downloader call sites. If DenyOutbound is active, any attempt to invoke a downloader returns a typed error immediately (no retrie...

title: Emit local audit/telemetry events for offline bundle verification outcomes (with redaction)
preview: Summary: This PR emits local audit/telemetry events during offline bundle signature+manifest verification. Events cover both success and failure paths with a stable schema and an explicit redaction policy suitable for air-gapped/regulated environments. The installer continues to make no outbound network calls; event...
text:
Summary: This PR emits local audit/telemetry events during offline bundle signature+manifest verification. Events cover both success and failure paths with a stable schema and an explicit redaction policy suitable for air-gapped/regulated environments. The installer continues to make no outbound network calls; events are written locally and can later be exported via the existing audit-log-exporter integration (when present) or collected via support bundles.

Motivation: Support and customer security teams asked for an auditable record of verification outcomes to answer: (1) did the installer verify the bundle? (2) why did it fail? (3) which trust root was used? and (4) did anyone use override/break-glass. Prior behavior only printed CLI output and wrote unstructured logs, which is hard to correlate and insufficient for security reviews.

Behavior: - On successful verification, emit ev...

title: Fix air-gapped time skew causing "certificate not yet valid" during bundle signature verification
preview: Context: Several pilot/on-prem installs in fully air-gapped environments hit preflight failures when verifying signed offline bundles with errors like `x509: certificate has expired or is not yet valid` (typically "not yet valid"). Root cause was cluster/installer host clocks being behind (RTC drift, no NTP, staged ...
text:
Context: Several pilot/on-prem installs in fully air-gapped environments hit preflight failures when verifying signed offline bundles with errors like `x509: certificate has expired or is not yet valid` (typically "not yet valid"). Root cause was cluster/installer host clocks being behind (RTC drift, no NTP, staged VM templates). Because signature verification is time-bound (cert validity + signature timestamp), the verifier correctly failed closed, but the UX was misleading and the remediation path was unclear.

What this PR changes:
- Improves detection and messaging for the specific "not yet valid" time-skew case: we now surface the current system time, the signing cert `NotBefore` value, and the computed skew, plus a clear remediation checklist (set clock / enable NTP source / validate VM template time).
- Adds a small skew tolerance window (default 10 minutes) to account for mino...

title: Support detached signature layout (manifest.json + manifest.sig) for offline bundles
preview: Context: As part of offline bundle signing for Redwood Private, we need a stable on-disk layout that works well with standard file transfer tooling (USB, secure courier, malware scanning gateways) and doesn’t require rewriting the manifest to “embed” signature metadata. This PR adds first-class support for detached ...
text:
Context: As part of offline bundle signing for Redwood Private, we need a stable on-disk layout that works well with standard file transfer tooling (USB, secure courier, malware scanning gateways) and doesn’t require rewriting the manifest to “embed” signature metadata. This PR adds first-class support for detached signatures and documents the expected bundle layout.

What’s changing: The installer now accepts either (a) detached signature layout: <bundle>/manifest.json + <bundle>/manifest.sig, or (b) the legacy/early layout where the signature is embedded in a single envelope file (supported for backwards compatibility during pilot). The verification preflight resolves the signature file by convention (manifest.sig alongside manifest.json) and fails closed if a signature is required but missing/unreadable.

Bundle layout (new recommended): - bundle-root/ - manifest.json (canonical JS...

title: Add signing trust store + basic key rollover support for offline bundles
preview: Context: Redwood Private offline bundles will be cryptographically signed and must be verifiable in air-gapped installs. We need a trust model that supports (a) multiple trusted signing keys (during rotation) and (b) a predictable way for customers to update trust roots in offline environments without rebuilding the...
text: Context: Redwood Private offline bundles will be cryptographically signed and must be verifiable in air-gapped installs. We need a trust model that supports (a) multiple trusted signing keys (during rotation) and (b) a predictable way for customers to update trust roots in offline environments without rebuilding the installer. This PR introduces a local trust store mechanism and implements basic key rollover support in the bundle verification path. Summary of changes: (1) Adds a trust store file format (JSON) that can contain multiple trusted public keys, each with a stable key_id (kid), algorithm, and optional metadata (created_at, not_before, not_after, comment). (2) Installer can load trust store keys from: default embedded trust store (compiled into the binary) OR an operator-provided trust store path (air-gapped friendly) OR an OS-level path (/etc/redwood-private/trust/signing-tr...

title: Add `bundle inspect` command for offline bundle diagnostics
preview:
Problem
Support/SE and on-prem operators currently have to run a full preflight (or hand-parse the bundle) to answer basic questions like: “What version is this bundle?”, “Is the manifest present?”, “Which signing key signed it?”, and “Did the offline transfer corrupt anything?”. For air-gapped environments, we need...
text:
Problem
Support/SE and on-prem operators currently have to run a full preflight (or hand-parse the bundle) to answer basic questions like: “What version is this bundle?”, “Is the manifest present?”, “Which signing key signed it?”, and “Did the offline transfer corrupt anything?”. For air-gapped environments, we need an offline-first diagnostic command that can be run on a laptop/jump host without starting an install.

What this PR does
Adds a new CLI subcommand: `redwood-private-installer bundle inspect <bundle-path>`.
The command is designed to be safe to run on a customer machine and produces a concise report that can be pasted into a Support ticket.

Capabilities
- Locate and load the bundle manifest (current: `bundle.manifest.json`; supports the existing legacy name `manifest.json` for pilot bundles).
- Validate manifest schema (required fields present; version fields parse; artif...

title: automated-az-switchover-playbooks-terraform-helm
preview: Motivation: automate multi-AZ switchover for Private deployments and encode DR runbooks as code so runbooks are executable, testable, and versioned. This PR introduces: 1) a Terraform module that wires up cross-AZ routing and health-check failover for Private clusters in customer VPCs; 2) Helm chart hooks and readin...
text: Motivation: automate multi-AZ switchover for Private deployments and encode DR runbooks as code so runbooks are executable, testable, and versioned. This PR introduces: 1) a Terraform module that wires up cross-AZ routing and health-check failover for Private clusters in customer VPCs; 2) Helm chart hooks and readiness probes to ensure stateful services (controller, kv-store) drain cleanly before leader evacuation; 3) Ansible/Playbook-driven 'switchover' runbooks (playbooks-as-code) for operators to execute or run during scheduled drills; 4) a safe rollback utility and CI e2e drill job to validate the full failover and restore path. Scope: supports AWS multi-AZ (subnet-awareness), mechanisms for on-prem inventory mapping (rack/az tags), and a new 'drill-runner' CI job that will run synthetic traffic against canary replicas and validate KV sync and stateful reattach. Implementation not...

title: Add canonical offline bundle manifest (hashes + artifact inventory) and wire into bundle build
preview: Context: Air-gapped / offline installs currently rely on “whatever is in the tarball” plus best-effort checksums produced by ad-hoc scripts. For offline signing + verification (follow-up PRs), the installer needs a deterministic, canonical manifest that enumerates bundle contents and pins hashes/versions. This PR in...
text:
Context: Air-gapped / offline installs currently rely on “whatever is in the tarball” plus best-effort checksums produced by ad-hoc scripts. For offline signing + verification (follow-up PRs), the installer needs a deterministic, canonical manifest that enumerates bundle contents and pins hashes/versions. This PR introduces a v0 manifest file at the root of each offline bundle, updates the bundle generation tooling to produce it deterministically, and adds unit/integration tests to prevent regressions.

What’s included:
- New file in bundle root: redwood-bundle-manifest.json (canonical JSON; stable key ordering and artifact list ordering)
- Manifest schema v0.1:
 - schema_version, created_at_utc, bundle_id (build id), redwood_private_version, installer_min_version
 - hash_algorithm (sha256)
 - artifacts[] inventory with: path, kind (image-tar|helm-chart|binary|config|model-artifact|sb...
